mechanism_narrative: A higher level of the federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically reducing equity present values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above its historical median, or the walk-forward Rank IC is greater than 0 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and consumption contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances after UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal's 2-year walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a coordinated demand-supply contraction that is not captured by either series alone, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO decline, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power with a delta R-squared below 0.01 in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of restrictive monetary policy, an acceleration in consumer price inflation forces the market to reprice terminal rate expectations higher, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases, or the conditional Rank IC is above 0.0 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Faster growth in industrial production signals expanding corporate output and operating leverage, which translates into positive earnings surprises and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below 1,500 thousand units signals a collapse in residential investment and forward-looking construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when HOUST is below 1500, or the signal fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.02 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment declines while the federal funds rate is elevated, the interaction of labor weakness with restrictive financial conditions amplifies recession risk beyond the independent effects of either variable, generating strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS above 3.5% does not produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 60% of instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10 in a 3-year rolling logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly real GDP growth signals an outright contraction in aggregate demand that depresses top-line revenue growth across the index, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth coincides with positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered quarters, or the signal's Rank IC is positive over any 3-year rolling window.
